steps.title: Recall the hemisphere volume formula
steps.content:
A hemisphere is half of a sphere. The volume of a sphere is $V_{sphere} = \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$, so the volume of a hemisphere is:
$$V_{hemisphere} = \frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3 = \frac{2}{3}\pi r^3$$

steps.title: Substitute r = 27 and calculate
steps.content:
With $r = 27$ inches:
$$V = \frac{2}{3}\pi (27)^3$$
First, calculate $27^3 = 27 \times 27 \times 27 = 729 \times 27 = 19,683$.

Then:
$$V = \frac{2}{3}\pi (19,683) = \frac{39,366}{3}\pi = 13,122\pi$$
Using $\pi \approx 3.14159$:
$$V \approx 13,122 \times 3.14159 \approx 41,235 \text{ cubic inches}$$
Among the choices, this is closest to 41,200. That matches choice D — D is correct.
